13. Maintain template versions; document analysis assumptions and interpretation guidance.
governanceConsiderations: Classify statistical outputs as Confidential if containing individual-level compensation or attrition data.
Implement RLS: restrict compensation equity reports to HR/Finance only.
Include statutory disclaimers: 'This analysis is correlational, not causal. Consult legal/HR before making employment decisions.'
Audit statistical test parameters and results: ensure tests are appropriately used (e.g., normality check before t-test).
Version-control analysis notebooks: track changes to statistical methods or assumptions.
Log all ad-hoc analysis execution: user, parameters, results, time/date.
Implement fact-checking: human review of statistical results before distribution to executives.
Document data limitations: e.g., 'Attrition drivers analysis excludes departures from M&A integration.'
Require HR/Legal approval before publishing findings related to pay equity or discrimination risks.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Attrition drivers analysis: logistic regression identifying salary, engagement, commute as attrition predictors.
Compensation equity audit: ANOVA testing for salary differences across gender, ethnicity, age controlling for role.
Engagement driver analysis: correlation analysis between survey engagement scores and external factors.
Org structure optimization: network analysis identifying span of control inefficiencies.
Talent market analysis: compare internal compensation distribution with external labor market benchmarks.
patterns.costImplications: Spark Notebook compute for statistical tests. Storage for output files (PDF, Excel, PowerPoint). Potential licensing for external statistical libraries.

patterns.name: LLM-Powered Analytical Narratives
patterns.summary: Uses Azure OpenAI/Fabric AI to generate natural language insights from data, summarizing trends and writing executive briefings with fact-grounding and hallucination checks.
patterns.description: This pattern leverages Azure OpenAI or Fabric's built-in AI capabilities to automatically generate natural language narratives from HR data and statistical analyses. LLM models are fine-tuned with HR domain context and given access to grounding data (facts extracted from Lakehouse tables). The system generates executive briefings summarizing compensation trends, engagement drivers, attrition forecasts, and organizational health. Guardrails prevent hallucinations: all claims must be traceable to underlying data; confidence scores indicate uncertainty; human-in-the-loop review gates high-stakes findings.
pros: LLM-generated narratives are more engaging than data visualizations alone.
Automated report writing saves analyst time on documentation.
Natural language makes insights accessible to non-technical executives.
Few-shot learning enables quick customization for HR domain.
Fact-grounding prevents speculation and improves credibility.
Handles ad-hoc questions: 'Why did attrition increase in Q3?'
cons: LLM hallucinations risk providing incorrect insights; requires robust fact-checking.
LLM interpretations may not align with causal domain knowledge (e.g., inferring causality from correlation).
Training on confidential HR data (salaries, attrition) poses privacy risks.
Regulatory uncertainty: legal implications of LLM-generated compliance findings unclear.
Cost: LLM API calls scale with analysis frequency.
User skepticism: 'Can we trust AI to understand our business?'
usageInstructions: 1. Set up Azure OpenAI instance with GPT-4 or GPT-3.5-turbo model.
2. Create Spark Notebook extracting 'fact tables' from Lakehouse: e.g., department headcount trends, salary distribution.
3. Implement fact-grounding: each claim in LLM output must reference a specific fact table value.
4. Build prompt engineering: design prompts that provide context, examples, and guardrails.
Example: 'You are an HR analytics expert. Generate a 2-paragraph executive brief on Q3 attrition. All claims must cite specific numbers from the data.'
5. Implement confidence scoring: use OpenAI API response probabilities to indicate uncertainty.
6. Create fact-checking layer: compare generated claims against ground truth (fact tables); flag mismatches.
7. Build human review workflow: route high-stakes findings (e.g., pay equity findings) to HR for approval.
8. Implement output formatting: markdown → PowerPoint or email briefing.
9. Create example templates: attrition narrative, compensation trend narrative, engagement narrative.
10. Schedule recurring narratives: e.g., weekly CEO briefing on org health.
11. Log all generated narratives and human review decisions for audits.
12. Implement feedback loop: track which narratives were actionable and refined model accordingly.
governanceConsiderations: Classify LLM-generated findings as draft until human review and approval.
Implement fact-checking: 100% validation that generated claims match ground truth data.
Audit LLM prompt inputs: ensure no raw sensitive data exposed to model training.
For regulated findings (pay equity, discrimination risk), require legal/HR approval before distribution.
Maintain audit trail: record prompt, model version, generated text, human review outcome.
Include disclaimers: 'Generated by AI; interpretation verified by HR Analytics team.'
Limit LLM access to anonymized/aggregated data; never pass individual employee records.
Establish guardrails preventing LLM from making causal claims on correlational data.
Monitor for bias: e.g., LLM should not infer causality for demographic differences.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Weekly CEO briefing: LLM summarizes org health (headcount, attrition, engagement) for executive update.
Compensation equity findings: LLM narrates pay gap analysis results in accessible language.
Attrition forecasting narrative: LLM explains top drivers of predicted attrition by department.
Survey feedback summarization: LLM generates themes from open-text survey responses.
Board reporting: LLM-generated commentary on workforce trends for shareholder communications.
prerequisites: Azure OpenAI deployment (or Fabric AI capabilities)
HR domain subject matter experts for prompt tuning
patterns.costImplications: Azure OpenAI API usage (per token). Fabric compute for fact extraction. Human review effort for governance. Optional fine-tuning costs.

patterns.name: Informatica Enterprise Data Catalog Integration
patterns.summary: Uses Informatica Enterprise Data Catalog (EDC) for automated metadata harvesting, data lineage visualization, and sensitive data discovery across Microsoft Fabric and external sources.
patterns.description: This pattern integrates Informatica Enterprise Data Catalog (EDC) as an enterprise metadata management and discovery platform that complements Microsoft Purview. EDC provides automated metadata extraction from Fabric Lakehouses, Warehouses, and external systems (Workday, SAP, databases) using native connectors. AI-powered lineage tracking visualizes end-to-end data flows from HR systems through Bronze/Silver/Gold layers to Power BI reports. Sensitive data discovery automatically identifies PII (employee names, SSNs, emails, phone numbers) across all registered data sources using pattern recognition and context analysis. Business glossary capabilities link technical metadata to business terms ("Active Employee", "Salary") enabling users to discover data by business concept rather than table names. Impact analysis shows downstream dependencies: if employee master data changes, users see which reports and analyses are affected.
pros: Automated metadata harvesting eliminates manual data dictionary maintenance.
AI-powered lineage tracks data transformations end-to-end across systems.
Sensitive data discovery identifies PII without manual scanning.
Cross-platform integration discovers data across Fabric, Workday, SAP, and custom databases.
Business glossary enables business users to find data by business concept.
Impact analysis reduces risk of unintended consequences from data model changes.
cons: Informatica EDC licensing is enterprise-focused with significant costs.
Initial metadata harvesting requires connector configuration for each data source.
Business glossary requires ongoing stewardship to maintain accuracy.
EDC and Purview have overlapping capabilities; organizations must decide governance model.
AI lineage learning may take time to achieve high accuracy with complex transformations.
usageInstructions: 1. Deploy Informatica EDC cloud instance with appropriate license tier.
2. Register Fabric Lakehouse, Warehouse, and OneLake sources in EDC catalog.
3. Configure EDC connectors for external systems: Workday, SAP, legacy databases.
4. Enable automated metadata scanning: schedule nightly harvesting of metadata changes.
5. Configure sensitive data discovery rules: identify columns matching SSN, email, phone patterns.
6. Build business glossary: define HR business terms (Active Employee, FTE, Salary Band).
7. Map technical attributes to business terms: link database columns to glossary.
8. Enable lineage tracing: configure Data Factory and Spark transformations for lineage capture.
9. Review auto-discovered lineage: validate and correct transformation paths if needed.
10. Configure impact analysis: enable analysis of downstream report dependencies.
11. Integrate EDC with Purview: surface EDC catalog in Fabric governance UI.
12. Create data discovery portal: expose EDC catalog to business users for self-service exploration.
governanceConsiderations: Define catalog stewardship roles: data owner for each domain (HR, Finance, Operations).
Establish sensitive data classification: which data requires encryption, masking, or access restrictions.
Monitor automated discovery accuracy: validate that lineage and PII detection match organizational data.
Maintain business glossary: assign business glossary managers to review and approve business terms.
Implement catalog security: restrict access to sensitive technical metadata based on roles.
Document lineage assumptions: clarify where AI lineage is inferred vs. explicit.
Establish approval workflows: require data owner sign-off on data catalog metadata.
Plan for metadata growth: monitor catalog performance as metadata volume increases.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Automated discovery of employee data across HR systems: find all employee tables in catalog.
Sensitive data mapping: identify all locations where SSN, salary, or health data exists.
Lineage tracking: understand how raw Workday data flows through transformations to compensation reports.
Impact analysis: determine which reports use employee data before modifying master data structure.
Business glossary: enable HR analyst to search for 'Active Employees' and discover all related data sources.
prerequisites: Informatica EDC subscription with appropriate license tier
Native connectors for Microsoft Fabric and target HR systems
Metadata extraction permissions on all source systems
patterns.costImplications: Informatica EDC licensing (enterprise tier). Metadata connector implementation. Business glossary stewardship and governance team overhead.

patterns.name: Informatica Axon Data Governance Framework
patterns.summary: Uses Informatica Axon for business-led data governance: data ownership assignment, policy management, stewardship workflows, and compliance tracking connecting business stakeholders to technical metadata.
patterns.description: This pattern implements Informatica Axon as a business-centric data governance platform that sits above technical tools like Purview and Fabric. Axon enables business leaders (data owners, stewards) to define data policies, assign ownership, and track compliance without requiring technical knowledge. Role-based activities assign stewardship responsibilities: VP of HR becomes 'owner' of employee data, compensation managers become 'stewards' for salary data. Axon workflows route governance requests (access approvals, data quality exceptions) to appropriate business owners with automated escalation. Impact scoring flags high-risk data changes: modifying employee master data is high-impact and triggers business approval. Policy management enforces rules (e.g., 'All salary data must be encrypted at rest') and tracks compliance status across systems. Axon bridges business policy ("We must protect employee privacy") with technical implementation (Fabric sensitivity labels, Purview classifications).
pros: Business-led governance: non-technical stakeholders can define policies and assign ownership.
Connects business policy to technical implementation through metadata mapping.
Stewardship workflows automate governance decisions with business owner sign-offs.
Impact scoring reduces risk by requiring approval of high-impact data changes.
Compliance tracking provides audit trails showing who approved what, when, and why.
Integrates with Fabric Purview for end-to-end governance visibility.
cons: Informatica Axon requires organizational change: business leaders must engage in governance.
Licensing costs for Axon SaaS platform on top of Informatica and Fabric expenses.
Workflow implementation requires careful role definition and approval matrix design.
Initial adoption slow if business leaders perceive governance as IT burden.
Integration with Purview and other tools requires custom connectors and data mapping.
usageInstructions: 1. Deploy Informatica Axon cloud platform with appropriate licensing.
2. Define organizational governance roles: data owner, steward, custodian, user.
3. Assign data owners: VP HR owns 'Employee Data', compensation manager owns 'Salary Data'.
4. Create stewardship teams: cross-functional teams responsible for specific data domains.
5. Define data policies: 'All salary data encrypted', 'Employee data retention 7 years', 'PII access logged'.
6. Configure stewardship workflows: approval chains for data access, policy exceptions, quality issues.
7. Map Axon policies to Fabric implementations: link 'Salary Encryption' policy to Fabric CMK configuration.
8. Set up impact assessment: define scoring for data change risk (master data changes = high).
9. Integrate with Purview: surface Axon ownership and policy status in Fabric governance UIs.
10. Create governance dashboard: show policy compliance status, pending approvals, audit trails.
11. Establish review cadence: quarterly policy reviews with data owners and stewards.
12. Build self-service data access portal: users request access, stewards approve, audit logged.
governanceConsiderations: Define clear governance roles: avoid confusion between owner, steward, custodian responsibilities.
Establish escalation procedures: define timelines for stewardship decisions (e.g., must approve within 5 days).
Document policy compliance evidence: maintain records showing which Fabric configurations implement which policies.
Monitor policy exceptions: track which rules are frequently waived and why (may indicate unrealistic policies).
Audit stewardship approvals: ensure decisions are made fairly and documented.
Plan for governance maturity: start with core policies, evolve as organization matures.
Communicate policies to users: train employees on which policies affect them and why.
Align with legal/compliance: ensure policies reflect regulatory requirements and organizational risk tolerance.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Data ownership: assign compensation manager as owner of salary data, enabling governance decisions.
Access approvals: steward approves analyst's access to employee performance data.
Policy enforcement: HR ensures all employee data complies with privacy policy requirements.
Impact assessment: identify downstream reports affected by employee master data structure change.
Compliance reporting: generate evidence that compensation data is handled per GDPR/CCPA requirements.
patterns.costImplications: Informatica Axon licensing (SaaS). Governance team training and change management. Integration services for Purview connectivity. Ongoing policy stewardship.

patterns.name: Informatica Data Quality for HR Data
patterns.summary: Uses Informatica Data Quality rules engine for HR data profiling, cleansing, standardization, and enrichment with ongoing quality metric monitoring.
patterns.description: This pattern implements Informatica Data Quality (DQ) for comprehensive HR data validation and cleansing. Data profiling analyzes employee tables (Workday extracts, legacy HRIS) to understand completeness, uniqueness, format compliance, and value distributions. Profiling identifies which fields have nulls (incomplete salary records), duplicates (duplicate employee IDs), or invalid formats (email without @). Cleansing rules standardize data: capitalize employee names, convert state abbreviations (CA -> California or keep short form consistently), format phone numbers (555-1234 -> 555-1234), normalize job titles ("Engineer" vs "Engg" vs "ENG"). Data enrichment adds missing values: validate employee ZIP code format, enriched location geography (region, country). Quality scorecards track metrics over time: percentage of employee records with valid email addresses, completeness of manager assignments, accuracy of employment status fields. Rules engine learns patterns and automatically detects anomalies: sudden spike in null salary fields triggers alerts. Monitoring dashboards show quality trends by source system (Workday vs legacy vs imports) enabling team to invest in highest-impact improvements.
pros: Comprehensive profiling identifies data quality issues before they impact analytics.
Standardization ensures consistent data formats (names, addresses, IDs) across sources.
Automated cleansing applies rules at scale without manual intervention.
Data enrichment adds missing values, improving analytics completeness.
Quality monitoring provides continuous visibility into data health trends.
Anomaly detection alerts teams to sudden data quality degradation.
Profiling-based rules adapt to data characteristics rather than static logic.
cons: Informatica DQ licensing is expensive, especially for large volumes.
Rule definition and maintenance requires data domain expertise (HR processes, data structures).
Cleansing rules must be carefully tuned to avoid masking legitimate data variance.
Initial profiling on large employee tables can be time-consuming.
Integration with Data Factory and Spark pipelines requires custom development.
usageInstructions: 1. Deploy Informatica Data Quality cloud instance.
2. Configure data connections to Workday, legacy HRIS, and Fabric Lakehouse Bronze layer.
3. Run comprehensive data profiling on employee master tables: analyze completeness, uniqueness, format.
4. Review profile results: identify high-priority quality issues (nulls in required fields, invalid formats).
5. Define cleansing rules: standardize names, addresses, phone numbers, job titles, employment status.
6. Create validation rules: enforce constraints (email format, salary > 0, valid department codes).
7. Build data enrichment rules: validate and add missing geography or organizational codes.
8. Configure quality scorecards: track percentage of records passing validation, completeness by field.
9. Integrate with Data Factory: embed DQ rules in ETL pipeline Bronze -> Silver transformation.
10. Set up quality monitoring: daily/weekly quality metrics dashboards by source system.
11. Create alerting: notify data team when quality metrics fall below thresholds.
12. Document cleansing decisions: maintain mapping of original vs. standardized values for audit.
governanceConsiderations: Define quality SLAs: e.g., '95% of employee records must pass validation to load to Silver layer'.
Maintain transformation audit trail: log which fields were cleansed, original vs. new values.
Balance cleansing rigor with accuracy: avoid over-aggressive cleansing that masks real data variance.
Align quality rules with business requirements: cleansing should reflect how data is actually used.
Monitor rule maintenance: rules must evolve as data sources and business processes change.
Classify data quality issues: separate critical (blocks load) from informational (may impact analysis).
Ensure PII protection: cleansing must preserve sensitivity labels and encryption.
Provide quality feedback to source systems: share profiling results with Workday/SAP teams.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Employee master data quality: ensure all required fields (name, ID, email, status) complete before analytics.
Compensation data standardization: ensure salary and job level data consistent across HR systems.
Location normalization: standardize office locations, enabling accurate geographic analysis.
Manager assignment validation: ensure each employee has exactly one manager, catching hierarchy errors.
Employment status consistency: ensure terminated employees are marked consistently across all tables.
prerequisites: Data connections to HR source systems (Workday, legacy HRIS)
Data profiling expertise or data steward resources
patterns.costImplications: Informatica DQ licensing (volume-based). Data profiling and rule definition services. Integration development for Data Factory pipelines.
patterns.connectorInfo: For Informatica DQ integration: use the IDMC cloud connector with Fabric Data Warehouse endpoint.

patterns.name: Informatica Data Privacy Management
patterns.summary: Uses Informatica Data Privacy Management (DPM) for automated PII discovery, data subject mapping, consent management, and privacy impact assessments supporting GDPR/CCPA/PIPEDA compliance.
patterns.description: This pattern implements Informatica Data Privacy Management (DPM) as an enterprise platform for privacy-by-design. Automated PII discovery scans Fabric Lakehouses, Warehouses, and external systems to identify personally identifiable information: employee names, SSNs, email addresses, phone numbers, home addresses, health/benefits data. Discovery uses pattern matching and contextual analysis (field names like 'ssn', 'email'; data patterns like XXX-XX-XXXX). Data mapping creates a subject data map showing all locations where employee data is stored and how it flows through systems. Subject Access Requests (SARs) are fulfilled by querying the map: user requests their data, DPM automatically finds all records across systems. Consent management tracks employee consents: has employee consented to marketing emails? Is their data used for ML model training? Privacy policies are enforced programmatically. Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs) analyze new projects: modifying compensation data triggers PIA to assess risks (can salary data be re-identified?). Integration with Fabric Purview and Microsoft Purview enables tagging all PII with 'confidential' sensitivity labels. Role Assignments specify which roles can access PII: only compensation managers can see salary, only benefits team sees health data.
pros: Automated PII discovery identifies sensitive data across all systems without manual scanning.
Data subject mapping enables quick fulfillment of access/deletion requests.
Consent management provides evidence of customer/employee consent for audits.
Privacy impact assessment framework reduces privacy risks in new projects.
Integration with Purview enables consistent privacy policy enforcement in Fabric.
Supports multiple regulations: GDPR, CCPA, PIPEDA, LGPD with region-specific rules.
Automated data deletion workflows enable GDPR 'right to be forgotten' compliance.
cons: Informatica DPM licensing is enterprise-focused with high costs for large deployments.
PII discovery rules must be carefully tuned to balance sensitivity and false positives.
Data subject mapping requires integration with identity systems (Entra ID, HR systems).
Privacy impact assessments require domain expertise in privacy regulations and risk.
Automated deletion workflows are complex and require careful testing before production use.
usageInstructions: 1. Deploy Informatica DPM cloud instance with appropriate licensing.
2. Register all data sources: Fabric Lakehouse, Warehouse, Workday, legacy HRIS, external databases.
3. Configure PII discovery rules: patterns for SSN, email, phone, address, health identifiers.
4. Run automated discovery: scan all registered sources for PII and sensitive data.
5. Review discovered PII: validate accuracy, refine patterns if too many false positives.
6. Map data subjects: link employee identities to all records containing their data across systems.
7. Tag PII in Purview: mark all discovered PII with 'confidential' sensitivity labels.
8. Define privacy policies: 'Employee data retained 3 years after termination', 'No salary data to third parties'.
9. Set up consent management: track which employees consented to what uses of their data.
10. Configure role-based access: ensure only authorized roles can access PII in Fabric.
11. Build SAR fulfillment workflow: create request form, DPM finds all subject data, provision secure download.
12. Create deletion workflows: define processes for removing data when rights-to-deletion requests arrive.
governanceConsiderations: Define privacy policies aligned with applicable regulations: GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), PIPEDA (Canada).
Maintain consent records: document what each employee consented to and when.
Establish PII discovery governance: assign roles for validating and categorizing discovered data.
Plan for privacy impact assessments: define triggers (new projects using employee data, system changes).
Document data retention: specify retention periods for each category of employee data.
Implement data deletion testing: test deletion workflows on non-production data before production use.
Audit role-based access: ensure only authorized roles see PII in Fabric.
Track regulatory changes: update policies and rules when regulations change (e.g., new privacy law).
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: PII mapping: identify all locations where employee SSNs, salaries, or health data are stored.
Subject access requests: fulfill employee requests for their data by querying privacy-mapped locations.
Consent tracking: verify employees consented before using their data in ML models or analytics.
Privacy compliance: ensure GDPR, CCPA, PIPEDA requirements are met in HR analytics practices.
Deletion workflows: fulfill 'right to be forgotten' requests by deleting all employee records on termination.
prerequisites: Connections to all data sources (Fabric, Workday, identity systems)
Entra ID or other identity system for subject mapping
patterns.costImplications: Informatica DPM licensing (enterprise tier). Integration services for data source connectivity. Privacy impact assessment consulting. Role-based access governance and testing.
patterns.connectorInfo: For Informatica DPM: use the IDMC connector for data masking and privacy management.

patterns.name: Dataiku DSS Advanced Analytics Platform
patterns.summary: Uses Dataiku DSS for visual ML pipeline development, collaborative data science, AutoML, model monitoring, and governed model deployment integrated with Fabric.
patterns.description: This pattern implements Dataiku DSS as an enterprise data science platform enabling both technical data scientists and business analysts to build, test, and deploy ML models. Visual workflow development eliminates low-level coding: analysts build ETL and ML pipelines by dragging components (read data, transform, train model, evaluate, deploy) rather than writing Python/Scala. AutoML capabilities automatically test multiple algorithms, feature engineering approaches, and hyperparameters to find best-performing models. Dataiku's built-in libraries provide pre-built models (churn prediction, clustering, anomaly detection) accelerating time-to-value. Collaborative notebooks enable team members to document reasoning and share insights. Model governance tracks which models are in production, who deployed them, performance metrics over time. Integration with Fabric via Spark and JDBC connectors enables Dataiku to read from Fabric Lakehouses and Warehouses as input data, and write trained models back to Fabric for serving. MLflow integration enables reproducibility and model registry. Monitoring dashboards track model performance: if employee churn model accuracy drops, alerts notify data science team to retrain.
pros: Visual workflow development enables business analysts to build models without coding.
AutoML automatically finds high-performing models, reducing expertise requirements.
Collaborative notebooks facilitate knowledge sharing and peer review.
Model governance provides audit trails for compliance and reproducibility.
Native integrations with Spark enable seamless Fabric data access.
Pre-built HR models (attrition, clustering, anomaly detection) accelerate deployment.
Model monitoring alerts on performance degradation requiring retraining.
cons: Dataiku DSS licensing is expensive, especially for large data volumes or user counts.
Visual workflow approach may be limiting for highly custom algorithms.
Requires discipline on model governance: easy to deploy unvetted models without controls.
Integration with Fabric requires custom Spark/JDBC connectors (Dataiku provides templates).
Learning curve for business users: visual workflows still require data science thinking.
usageInstructions: 1. Deploy Dataiku DSS instance on cloud or on-premises infrastructure.
2. Configure connections to Fabric Lakehouse and Warehouse as data sources.
3. Create project for HR analytics (e.g., Employee Churn Prediction).
4. Load training data: read employee history from Fabric Lakehouse Gold layer.
5. Build data preparation workflow: handle missing values, create features (tenure, performance score).
6. Explore data: visualizations of feature distributions, correlations with target (churned yes/no).
7. Divide into train/test/validation sets: 60% training, 20% test, 20% validation.
8. Run AutoML: Dataiku tests logistic regression, random forest, gradient boosting, neural networks.
9. Evaluate models: review accuracy, precision, recall, AUC metrics on test set.
10. Review model interpretation: understand which features drive predictions (tenure, low salary = high churn risk).
11. Deploy winning model: push to MLflow registry, create REST API endpoint.
12. Set up monitoring: track model performance on new data weekly, alert if accuracy drops below threshold.
governanceConsiderations: Establish model governance gates: require business sponsor approval before production deployment.
Document model assumptions: which employee segments is model valid for? When should it be retrained?.
Maintain feature lineage: track which Fabric tables/columns feed model inputs.
Monitor model fairness: track prediction accuracy across demographic groups (gender, race, age).
Set up retraining triggers: define when model must be retrained (monthly? quarterly? or if accuracy drops X%).
Create model versioning: maintain version history with performance metrics for each version.
Implement access controls: restrict model development to vetted data scientists, restrict deployment to trained team.
Plan for model obsolescence: define rules for retiring underperforming models.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Employee churn prediction: build model predicting which employees likely to leave in 6 months.
Performance prediction: predict employee performance ratings based on historical patterns.
Compensation clustering: identify groups of employees with similar compensation vs. market.
Anomaly detection: flag employees with unusual attendance, leave, or compensation patterns.
Succession planning: identify high-potential employees likely for advancement.
prerequisites: Dataiku DSS subscription with appropriate license tier
Spark and JDBC connectors configured for Fabric connectivity
Training data prepared in Fabric Lakehouse Gold layer
patterns.costImplications: Dataiku DSS licensing (SaaS, volume-based). Data scientist onboarding and training. Spark compute costs for model training. MLflow integration and monitoring infrastructure.

patterns.name: Apache Airflow Pipeline Orchestration
patterns.summary: Uses Apache Airflow for DAG-based workflow orchestration of complex multi-step ETL pipelines across Fabric and external systems, with scheduling, dependency management, retry logic, and SLA monitoring.
patterns.description: This pattern implements Apache Airflow as an open-source workflow orchestration platform for managing complex multi-step data pipelines. DAGs (Directed Acyclic Graphs) define pipeline dependencies: 'Extract data from Workday' must complete before 'Transform employee data', which must complete before 'Load to Gold layer', which must complete before 'Refresh Power BI'. Airflow schedules pipelines using cron expressions ("every day at 2am"), triggers conditions ("when Workday export file arrives"), or manual kicks. Task dependencies ensure work happens in correct order; Airflow parallelizes independent tasks automatically (three employee data sources can extract in parallel). Retry logic automatically reruns failed tasks (e.g., if network timeout, retry up to 3 times). SLA monitoring alerts on missed deadlines: if daily Workday ETL doesn't complete by 8am, notify data team. Airflow UI provides real-time visibility into job status, logs, and performance. Sensors monitor external events (has file arrived? has API returned data?). Integration with Fabric via custom operators or standard HTTP/Python operators enables triggering Data Factory pipelines, querying Spark jobs, updating Warehouse tables. Alternative/complement to Data Factory for teams already invested in Airflow or managing pipelines across multiple cloud providers.
pros: DAG-based approach provides clear visibility into pipeline dependencies and execution order.
Scheduling via cron and custom triggers provides flexible workflow timing.
Automatic retry logic reduces manual intervention for transient failures.
SLA monitoring alerts on missed deadlines before they impact downstream processes.
Parallelization of independent tasks reduces overall pipeline execution time.
Rich UI provides real-time job status, logs, and performance metrics.
Open-source platform with large ecosystem of community operators and connectors.
Works across clouds: can orchestrate pipelines in AWS, Azure, GCP in single platform.
cons: Airflow requires operational infrastructure: database, executor, scheduler must be kept running.
Learning curve: DAGs require Python coding; teams must learn Airflow concepts.
Scaling Airflow to thousands of pipelines requires careful infrastructure planning.
Data Factory integration requires custom operators or HTTP calls (not as tightly integrated as Azure-native).
Operational overhead: monitoring, logging, alerting must be configured and maintained.
usageInstructions: 1. Deploy Apache Airflow: cloud-managed (Composer, MWAA) or self-hosted.
2. Define DAG for HR ETL pipeline (Python file with @dag decorator).
3. Create tasks for each step: ExtractWorkday, TransformEmployee, LoadGoldLayer, RefreshPowerBI.
4. Define task dependencies: ExtractWorkday >> TransformEmployee >> LoadGoldLayer >> RefreshPowerBI.
5. Configure scheduling: @dag(schedule='0 2 * * *') = daily at 2am local time.
6. Add retry logic: Task(retries=3, retry_delay=timedelta(minutes=5)).
7. Set SLA: @dag(sla=timedelta(hours=6)) = pipeline must complete within 6 hours.
8. Configure sensors: FileSensor to wait for Workday export file before starting.
9. Add Data Factory integration: HttpOperator to trigger Data Factory pipeline.
10. Implement error handling: on_failure_callback to notify team on task failure.
11. Set up monitoring: configure alerts for SLA breaches, failed tasks, long-running jobs.
12. Test DAG: use Airflow CLI to validate syntax, test individual tasks locally.
governanceConsiderations: Document DAG purpose and owner: each pipeline should have documented business purpose and owner.
Maintain versioning: track DAG changes, enable rollback if update causes issues.
Monitor resource usage: Airflow execution logs must show actual runtime for capacity planning.
Audit task changes: implement code review process for pipeline changes before deployment.
Set realistic SLAs: base on actual historical performance, not wishful thinking.
Plan for growth: design scalability; Airflow can struggle with thousands of pipelines.
Document troubleshooting: maintain runbooks for common failures (API timeout, file not found).
Establish on-call support: define who responds to SLA breaches and failed pipelines overnight.
peopleAnalyticsUseCases: Daily employee data ETL: orchestrate multi-step pipeline from Workday extraction through Gold layer refresh.
Payroll processing: schedule complex transformations (tax calculations, deduction processing) with SLA monitoring.
Organizational network analysis: orchestrate graph database updates from org hierarchy data.
Cross-border compliance: manage multi-step pipelines respecting data residency constraints.
Real-time analytics: trigger updates when employee status changes (hire, promotion, termination).
prerequisites: Network connectivity to Fabric and external systems
patterns.costImplications: Airflow infrastructure (cloud-managed like Composer or self-hosted VMs). Compute for pipeline execution. Operational overhead for monitoring and support.
patterns.connectorInfo: Requires Apache Airflow. For Fabric integration: install the apache-airflow-microsoft-fabric-plugin and use FabricRunItemOperator.

useCaseBlueprints.name: Attrition Risk Scoring & Fairness Monitoring
useCaseBlueprints.description: Weekly ML-based attrition risk scoring that identifies high-risk employees before they leave. Incorporates fairness monitoring to ensure predictions are unbiased across demographics, with automated alerts to managers for at-risk talent.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Reduces unwanted attrition by enabling proactive retention interventions. Fairness monitoring ensures equity in talent management decisions across all employee demographics, reducing legal and reputational risk.

patternFlow.role: Establishes Bronze-Silver-Gold data layers for clean, aggregated HR data
patternFlow.dataFlow: Raw HR systems → Bronze → enriched employee records → Silver

patternFlow.role: Creates reusable ML features (tenure, engagement, salary growth) for model training
patternFlow.dataFlow: Silver layer → feature engineering → feature store with Delta versioning

patternFlow.role: Runs weekly ML inference to score attrition risk for all employees
patternFlow.dataFlow: Feature store → ML model → risk scores per employee

patternFlow.role: Evaluates predictions across demographic groups to identify and flag bias
patternFlow.dataFlow: Risk scores + demographics → fairness metrics → bias report

patternFlow.role: Restricts manager access to only their direct reports' risk scores
patternFlow.dataFlow: Risk scores filtered by manager org hierarchy in semantic layer

patternFlow.role: Enables real-time Power BI dashboards on risk scores with no copy
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold layer tables → DirectLake → Power BI dashboards

patternFlow.role: Triggers automated alerts when risk scores exceed thresholds
patternFlow.dataFlow: Risk scores → Reflex rules → alerts to HR and managers

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint orchestrates a complete ML-powered attrition prevention system. Employee data flows through the medallion architecture's Bronze and Silver layers where it's enriched and deduplicated. The feature store then engineers critical ML features (tenure, compensation trends, engagement indicators, promotion history) that feed into a batch inference pipeline running weekly predictions. Every prediction cycle passes through fairness evaluation to catch demographic bias before results reach stakeholders. Row-level security ensures managers see only their team's scores, while DirectLake provides instant dashboard access. Data Activator closes the loop by pushing real-time alerts when risk thresholds are breached.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Model validation and testing for bias before deployment, Quarterly fairness audits across protected classes, Data residency compliance for sensitive PII in feature store, Access logging for all users viewing risk scores, Regular model retraining and performance monitoring, Documentation of fairness thresholds and intervention triggers
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 8-12 weeks for initial deployment including model training and fairness validation

useCaseBlueprints.name: Automated Regulatory Reporting
useCaseBlueprints.description: Automates the generation of regulatory compliance reports including EEO-1, VETS-4212, AAP, ACA, OSHA, and state/local workforce disclosures. Validates data quality, applies required anonymization, generates paginated reports in mandated formats, and enforces data loss prevention policies throughout.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Eliminates manual report compilation that typically consumes weeks of analyst time per filing cycle. Ensures data accuracy and consistency across regulatory submissions, reduces compliance risk from missed deadlines or erroneous filings, and creates an auditable trail for all regulatory data transformations.

patternFlow.role: Structures raw HR data into progressively refined layers for regulatory consumption
patternFlow.dataFlow: HRIS/payroll/benefits systems → Bronze → Silver → Gold regulatory tables

patternFlow.role: Validates completeness and accuracy of all fields required by each regulatory form
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold tables → validation rules → quality scorecards → flagged exceptions

patternFlow.role: Applies statistical anonymization where regulations require aggregate-only reporting
patternFlow.dataFlow: Employee-level data → k-anonymity grouping → anonymized aggregates

patternFlow.role: Generates pixel-perfect reports matching mandated regulatory formats (EEO-1, VETS, etc.)
patternFlow.dataFlow: Validated Gold data → paginated report templates → formatted submissions

patternFlow.role: Classifies regulatory data with appropriate sensitivity labels for handling
patternFlow.dataFlow: Report artifacts → auto-classification → labeled documents

patternFlow.role: Prevents unauthorized sharing or export of regulatory filing data
patternFlow.dataFlow: Labeled documents → DLP policies → controlled distribution

patternFlow.role: Logs all data access and report generation for regulatory audit trails
patternFlow.dataFlow: All system events → audit logs → SIEM for compliance monitoring

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint creates a fully automated regulatory reporting pipeline. Raw workforce data from HRIS, payroll, and benefits systems flows through the medallion architecture into regulatory-specific Gold tables that map directly to filing requirements. Data quality validation catches missing or inconsistent fields before they reach reports. Where regulations require aggregate reporting, k-anonymity ensures individual privacy. Paginated Reports generates submission-ready documents in mandated formats. Sensitivity labels and DLP policies protect the regulatory data throughout its lifecycle, while comprehensive audit logging satisfies regulatory examination requirements.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Annual review of regulatory filing requirements and report templates, Data quality thresholds per regulatory form with exception handling procedures, Anonymization parameter tuning per regulation (e.g., minimum group sizes), Access restricted to authorized compliance personnel only, Audit log retention matching regulatory record-keeping requirements, Version control for all report templates and transformation logic
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 6-10 weeks including template development for each regulatory form and validation rule configuration

useCaseBlueprints.name: Conversational HR Analytics with Secure Retrieval
useCaseBlueprints.description: A secure, AI-powered conversational interface that allows HR professionals and managers to ask natural language questions about workforce data. Uses retrieval-augmented generation grounded in Fabric data with row-level security enforcement, ensuring users only see data they're authorized to access.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Democratizes access to HR analytics by removing the need for technical query skills. Managers get instant answers about their team metrics while security controls prevent unauthorized data access. Reduces HR analytics team bottleneck for ad-hoc data requests by 60-80%.

patternFlow.role: Provides clean, trusted Gold-layer data for AI retrieval
patternFlow.dataFlow: HR systems → Bronze → Silver → Gold analytics tables

patternFlow.role: Creates a governed semantic layer that the AI interface queries against
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold tables → certified semantic model → single source of truth

patternFlow.role: Grounds AI responses in actual Fabric data rather than hallucinated content
patternFlow.dataFlow: User question → vector search → relevant data chunks → grounded response

patternFlow.role: Enforces row-level security so users only retrieve their authorized data
patternFlow.dataFlow: User identity → RLS filter → scoped data retrieval → filtered response

patternFlow.role: Prevents the AI from generating inappropriate, biased, or non-compliant responses
patternFlow.dataFlow: AI response → guardrail checks → safe, compliant answer

patternFlow.role: Logs all queries and responses for compliance review and model improvement
patternFlow.dataFlow: All interactions → audit log → SIEM dashboard → compliance review

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint delivers a secure conversational analytics experience built on Fabric's AI capabilities. Clean, trusted data from the medallion Gold layer feeds into a certified semantic model that serves as the AI's knowledge base. RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) ensures every AI response is grounded in real data. Row-level security enforcement means the chatbot automatically scopes responses to what each user is authorized to see. HR-specific AI guardrails prevent inappropriate outputs, and comprehensive audit logging creates a full trail of every interaction for compliance review.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Regular testing of RLS enforcement in conversational context, AI response quality monitoring and bias auditing, Guardrail rule updates as HR policies change, User feedback collection for continuous improvement, Quarterly review of audit logs for anomalous query patterns, Clear documentation of what the AI can and cannot answer
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 10-14 weeks including AI model tuning, guardrail configuration, and user acceptance testing

useCaseBlueprints.name: Complete Employee Lifecycle Tracking
useCaseBlueprints.description: End-to-end tracking of every employee touchpoint from recruitment through offboarding. Captures hire, onboarding milestones, role changes, performance cycles, compensation adjustments, learning completions, and separation events in a unified timeline with slowly changing dimension history preservation.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Creates a single source of truth for the complete employee journey, enabling HR to identify patterns across the lifecycle (e.g., onboarding gaps correlating with early attrition). Supports both operational reporting and strategic workforce analysis with full historical context.

patternFlow.role: Captures new and changed employee events incrementally from source systems
patternFlow.dataFlow: HRIS/ATS/LMS/Perf systems → watermark-based incremental loads → Bronze

patternFlow.role: Preserves full history of every employee attribute change over time
patternFlow.dataFlow: Bronze events → SCD Type 2 processing → historical dimension tables

patternFlow.role: Organizes lifecycle data into progressive refinement layers
patternFlow.dataFlow: Raw events → Bronze → cleansed Silver → analytics-ready Gold

patternFlow.role: Validates lifecycle events for completeness, consistency, and timeliness
patternFlow.dataFlow: Silver data → validation rules → quality scores → exception alerts

patternFlow.role: Isolates lifecycle data domains while enabling cross-domain analysis
patternFlow.dataFlow: Domain workspaces (recruit, perform, learn) → hub workspace → unified view

patternFlow.role: Delivers real-time lifecycle dashboards without data duplication
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold lifecycle tables → DirectLake → Power BI lifecycle dashboards

patternFlow.role: Tracks lifecycle KPIs (time-to-fill, onboarding completion, retention rates)
patternFlow.dataFlow: Lifecycle metrics → Power BI scorecards → goal tracking → alerts

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint builds a comprehensive employee lifecycle data platform. Incremental loading captures changes from multiple HR systems as they happen. SCD Type 2 processing preserves the full history of every employee attribute — so you can always see what someone's role, manager, and compensation were at any point in time. The medallion architecture refines raw events into analytics-ready Gold tables. Data quality validation catches gaps in the lifecycle chain. Hub-spoke workspace design keeps domain data organized while enabling cross-domain analysis. DirectLake dashboards provide instant access to lifecycle views, and metrics scorecards track key lifecycle KPIs against organizational goals.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Data classification and sensitivity labeling for all lifecycle stages, Retention policies aligned with employment law requirements per jurisdiction, Access controls differentiated by lifecycle stage (e.g., compensation vs. performance), Audit trail for all lifecycle data modifications, Regular reconciliation against source systems to ensure completeness
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 10-16 weeks depending on number of source systems and complexity of historical data migration

useCaseBlueprints.name: Privacy Compliance & Data Subject Request Fulfillment
useCaseBlueprints.description: End-to-end privacy compliance framework covering data discovery, classification, retention management, and automated data subject request (DSR) fulfillment for GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, PIPEDA, and other privacy regulations. Provides complete visibility into where personal data resides and automates the response process for access, deletion, and portability requests.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Reduces DSR response time from weeks to hours, ensures consistent compliance across jurisdictions, and minimizes legal exposure from missed deadlines or incomplete fulfillment. Creates a defensible privacy posture with comprehensive audit trails.

patternFlow.role: Discovers and catalogs all personal data across the Fabric environment
patternFlow.dataFlow: All data sources → automated scanning → data map with PII locations

patternFlow.role: Classifies discovered data by sensitivity level and applicable regulations
patternFlow.dataFlow: Data map → auto-classification → sensitivity labels applied

patternFlow.role: Prevents unauthorized movement or sharing of classified personal data
patternFlow.dataFlow: Labeled data → DLP policy evaluation → blocked/allowed actions

patternFlow.role: Automates retention and purge schedules based on regulatory requirements
patternFlow.dataFlow: Classified data → retention rules → scheduled purge → deletion confirmation

patternFlow.role: Processes data subject requests (access, delete, portability) across all systems
patternFlow.dataFlow: DSR intake → data discovery → fulfillment actions → response generation

patternFlow.role: Creates tamper-proof audit trail of all privacy actions and decisions
patternFlow.dataFlow: All privacy events → audit logs → SIEM → compliance dashboards

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint implements a comprehensive privacy compliance framework. Purview Data Map provides continuous discovery of where personal data resides across the Fabric environment. Sensitivity labels automatically classify data based on content inspection and applicable regulations. DLP policies enforce handling rules that prevent unauthorized sharing. Automated retention management ensures data is purged according to regulatory schedules. When data subject requests arrive, the DSR fulfillment pipeline automatically locates all relevant data, executes the requested action (access, deletion, or portability), and generates the compliance response. Every action is logged to SIEM for a complete audit trail.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Privacy impact assessments for new data processing activities, Regular data map refresh to catch new PII locations, DSR SLA monitoring (30 days GDPR, 45 days CCPA), Retention schedule review annually or when regulations change, Cross-border data transfer impact assessments, Privacy team training on system capabilities and limitations
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 8-12 weeks for core framework, plus 2-4 weeks per additional jurisdiction's specific requirements

useCaseBlueprints.name: ML-Powered Workforce Planning & Headcount Forecasting
useCaseBlueprints.description: Machine learning-driven workforce planning that forecasts headcount needs, identifies skill gaps, and models scenario-based workforce strategies. Uses historical workforce data, business growth projections, and external market signals to generate predictive forecasts with automated drift detection to ensure model accuracy over time.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Transforms workforce planning from reactive spreadsheet exercises into proactive, data-driven strategy. Enables HR and Finance to align on headcount investments 6-12 months ahead, reducing emergency hiring costs and improving talent pipeline readiness.

patternFlow.role: Structures historical workforce and business data for ML consumption
patternFlow.dataFlow: HRIS + Finance + market data → Bronze → Silver → Gold ML-ready tables

patternFlow.role: Engineers and versions features like growth rates, attrition trends, skill distributions
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold tables → feature engineering → versioned feature store

patternFlow.role: Tracks, versions, and manages all forecasting models in a central registry
patternFlow.dataFlow: Trained models → MLflow registry → model versioning → deployment

patternFlow.role: Runs periodic forecasting inference across all business units
patternFlow.dataFlow: Feature store + registered model → batch predictions → forecast outputs

patternFlow.role: Monitors forecast accuracy and triggers retraining when predictions degrade
patternFlow.dataFlow: Predictions vs actuals → drift metrics → alerts → retraining triggers

patternFlow.role: Visualizes forecasts, scenarios, and variance analysis in interactive dashboards
patternFlow.dataFlow: Forecast outputs → DirectLake → Power BI planning dashboards

patternFlow.role: Alerts leadership when forecasts indicate critical staffing gaps or surpluses
patternFlow.dataFlow: Forecast thresholds → Reflex rules → alerts to HR and Finance leaders

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint builds an intelligent workforce planning engine. Historical workforce data, financial projections, and market signals flow through the medallion architecture into ML-ready Gold tables. The feature store engineers predictive features — growth rates, attrition velocity, skill distribution shifts — that feed into forecasting models managed through MLflow. Batch inference runs periodic predictions across all business units and roles. Model drift detection continuously monitors forecast accuracy against actual outcomes, triggering retraining when performance degrades. Interactive Power BI dashboards let planners explore forecasts and run what-if scenarios, while Data Activator pushes alerts when critical staffing thresholds are approaching.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Model validation before production deployment with documented accuracy metrics, Quarterly forecast-vs-actual reconciliation reviews, Feature store access controls to prevent unauthorized feature modification, Model drift thresholds documented and reviewed semi-annually, Scenario modeling assumptions documented for audit trail, Finance-HR alignment meetings to validate forecast inputs
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 12-16 weeks including historical data preparation, model development, and stakeholder calibration

useCaseBlueprints.name: Real-Time Organizational Health Monitoring & Automated Escalation
useCaseBlueprints.description: Continuous monitoring of organizational health indicators — engagement scores, attrition spikes, absence patterns, overtime trends, and survey sentiment — with automated escalation when metrics breach defined thresholds. Provides executives with a real-time pulse on workforce health across all business units.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Shifts organizational health monitoring from lagging quarterly reviews to real-time awareness. Automated escalation ensures critical workforce issues (sudden attrition spikes, engagement drops, compliance risks) reach the right leaders within hours rather than weeks, enabling rapid intervention.

patternFlow.role: Captures workforce changes in near-real-time as they happen in source systems
patternFlow.dataFlow: HRIS/survey/time systems → CDC streams → real-time event capture

patternFlow.role: Processes and refines streaming events into analytics-ready health metrics
patternFlow.dataFlow: CDC events → Bronze → Silver → Gold health metric tables

patternFlow.role: Validates health metrics for accuracy before they drive alerts
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold metrics → validation rules → quality-assured metrics

patternFlow.role: Displays organizational health KPIs with goal tracking and trend analysis
patternFlow.dataFlow: Validated metrics → Power BI scorecards → executive health dashboard

patternFlow.role: Monitors metrics continuously and triggers alerts when thresholds are breached
patternFlow.dataFlow: Health metrics → threshold rules → triggered alerts

patternFlow.role: Orchestrates escalation workflows including notifications and task creation
patternFlow.dataFlow: Triggered alerts → Power Automate → emails, Teams messages, tasks

patternFlow.role: Routes escalations to the correct leadership level based on severity and scope
patternFlow.dataFlow: Alert severity + org scope → routing rules → appropriate leader notification

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint creates a real-time organizational health nervous system. Change data capture streams workforce events as they happen — terminations, survey submissions, absence records, overtime entries. These flow through the medallion architecture for rapid processing into health metrics. Data quality validation prevents false alarms from bad data. Power BI scorecards give executives an always-current view of organizational health. Data Activator continuously monitors metrics against defined thresholds. When breaches occur, Power Automate orchestrates the escalation workflow — sending the right notification to the right person at the right level of leadership based on the severity and organizational scope of the issue.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Threshold definitions reviewed quarterly with executive stakeholders, Escalation routing rules updated when organizational structure changes, False positive monitoring to prevent alert fatigue, Data quality SLAs for real-time source systems, Privacy review of health metrics to ensure no individual identification in executive views, Escalation response SLAs tracked and reported
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 8-12 weeks including threshold calibration, escalation workflow design, and executive dashboard development

useCaseBlueprints.name: Secure Compensation Analytics & Market Benchmarking
useCaseBlueprints.description: Enables compensation analysis and market benchmarking while protecting individual salary details through k-anonymity and dynamic data masking. Allows cross-department sharing of anonymized salary insights for equitable pay decisions without exposing sensitive individual compensation data.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Provides data-driven compensation decisions while maintaining strict privacy. Enables pay equity analysis, market competitiveness benchmarking, and budget modeling without exposing individual salaries. Reduces pay equity litigation risk through documented, data-driven processes.

patternFlow.role: Structures compensation data from HRIS and survey sources into analysis-ready layers
patternFlow.dataFlow: HRIS/payroll/survey data → Bronze → Silver → Gold comp tables

patternFlow.role: Preserves full compensation history for trend analysis and equity audits
patternFlow.dataFlow: Compensation changes → SCD Type 2 → historical salary dimension

patternFlow.role: Anonymizes compensation data for cross-department sharing and benchmarking
patternFlow.dataFlow: Individual comp data → k-anonymity grouping → anonymized aggregates

patternFlow.role: Masks individual salaries in real-time based on viewer authorization level
patternFlow.dataFlow: Comp queries → dynamic masking rules → masked/unmasked results

patternFlow.role: Restricts compensation visibility to authorized scope (own team, own department)
patternFlow.dataFlow: User identity → RLS rules → scoped compensation data

patternFlow.role: Provides a single governed source for all compensation metrics and definitions
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold comp tables → certified semantic model → consistent metrics

patternFlow.role: Delivers interactive compensation dashboards with embedded security controls
patternFlow.dataFlow: Semantic model → DirectLake → Power BI comp dashboards

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint secures the full compensation analytics lifecycle. Raw compensation data from HRIS and payroll systems flows through the medallion architecture with SCD Type 2 processing to preserve complete salary history. K-anonymity ensures that any shared compensation data cannot be traced to individuals. Dynamic data masking provides real-time protection — analysts see masked values while authorized comp team members see actuals. Row-level security scopes visibility to each user's authorized population. A certified semantic model ensures everyone uses the same metric definitions, and DirectLake dashboards deliver interactive analytics with all security controls enforced in real-time.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Minimum group sizes for anonymized reporting (typically 5-10 employees), Dynamic masking rules reviewed when roles or access policies change, Pay equity analysis methodology documented for legal defensibility, Compensation data access logged and reviewed quarterly, Market benchmarking data sourcing and refresh cadence documented, Audit trail for all compensation decision support queries
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 8-10 weeks including anonymization parameter tuning and security configuration

useCaseBlueprints.name: Analytic Sandbox
useCaseBlueprints.description: A governed self-service environment where HR analysts and data scientists can upload ad-hoc datasets, combine them with trusted enterprise data, and perform statistical analysis, machine learning, and generative AI exploration. Provides Spark notebooks, Python/R environments, and LLM-powered analytical tools within a secure, isolated workspace that prevents data leakage while enabling creative exploration.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Empowers HR analytics teams to rapidly test hypotheses and explore data without waiting for IT pipeline development. Reduces ad-hoc analysis turnaround from weeks to hours. Provides a governed alternative to shadow analytics (unauthorized spreadsheets and personal tools) while maintaining data security standards.

patternFlow.role: Provides a governed workspace for analysts to upload and manage ad-hoc datasets
patternFlow.dataFlow: CSV/Excel/JSON uploads → validated staging area → sandbox Lakehouse

patternFlow.role: Provides trusted enterprise data that analysts can join with their uploaded data
patternFlow.dataFlow: Enterprise Gold tables available as read-only references in sandbox

patternFlow.role: Gives analysts Spark notebooks for statistical analysis and data transformation
patternFlow.dataFlow: Sandbox + enterprise data → Spark notebooks → analysis outputs

patternFlow.role: Enables point-and-click insight generation for non-technical users
patternFlow.dataFlow: Selected data → automated analysis → insight summaries and visualizations

patternFlow.role: Provides generative AI tools for natural language querying and narrative generation
patternFlow.dataFlow: Analyst questions → LLM analysis → narrative insights and recommendations

patternFlow.role: Ensures analysts can only access enterprise data within their authorization scope
patternFlow.dataFlow: Analyst identity → RLS rules → scoped enterprise data access

patternFlow.role: Auto-classifies uploaded data and prevents sensitive data from leaving the sandbox
patternFlow.dataFlow: Uploaded data → content inspection → sensitivity labels → handling rules

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint creates a secure analytical playground. The sandbox workspace provides a governed upload area where analysts can bring in ad-hoc datasets — survey exports, external benchmarks, project data — and combine them with trusted enterprise Gold layer data. Spark notebooks offer full Python/R/SQL capabilities for statistical modeling and data science. The ad-hoc insight engine provides a lower-code option for quick exploration. LLM-powered analysis tools allow natural language querying and automated narrative generation. Security is maintained through row-level security on enterprise data and sensitivity labels that auto-classify uploaded content and enforce handling rules, preventing accidental data leakage from the sandbox.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Sandbox data retention policies (auto-purge after 90 days unless promoted), Upload size limits and prohibited file type enforcement, Enterprise data access scoped by analyst role and department, Sensitivity label policies for uploaded data auto-classification, Output review process before sandbox insights are shared externally, Resource quotas to prevent Spark cluster abuse
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 6-8 weeks for sandbox infrastructure, security configuration, and tool provisioning

useCaseBlueprints.name: Document & Presentation Creation
useCaseBlueprints.description: Leverages Fabric's data, AI, and automation capabilities to generate high-quality, template-following HR documents and presentations. Combines certified data from semantic models with LLM-generated narratives and Power BI visuals to auto-produce board decks, talent reviews, compensation summaries, workforce planning presentations, and compliance reports that adhere to organizational templates and branding standards.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Eliminates hours of manual copy-paste-format work for recurring HR deliverables. Ensures documents always use current, certified data and consistent formatting. Enables HR teams to produce executive-quality materials in minutes rather than days, with full data lineage and accuracy.

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint automates HR document and presentation production. Certified semantic models ensure every document starts from trusted, governed data. Power BI generates the charts and visualizations. LLM-powered narrative generation produces executive summaries, commentary, and analysis text that contextualize the data. Paginated Reports assembles everything into template-following documents — whether that's a board deck, talent review packet, or compensation summary. Power Automate orchestrates the workflow: triggering assembly on schedule, combining components, and distributing the finished product. Sensitivity labels protect generated documents, and deployment pipelines manage template versioning so updates are tested before reaching production.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Template approval workflow before production deployment, LLM output review process for generated narratives (human-in-the-loop), Certified data source validation — documents must only use certified semantic models, Branding and formatting standards enforced through locked template sections, Distribution lists reviewed quarterly, Version control for all document templates with change log
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 6-10 weeks including template design, LLM prompt engineering, and workflow automation setup

useCaseBlueprints.name: Real Estate Transaction Workflow Automation
useCaseBlueprints.description: Automates the real estate transaction lifecycle by extracting key information from lease agreements and checklist data, auto-populating required Excel forms (rent rolls, lease abstracts, approval matrices), orchestrating digital signature collection, and managing document archiving. Reduces manual data entry, accelerates transaction processing, and ensures compliance with real estate approval workflows.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Reduces lease processing time by 60-80% through automated data extraction and form population. Eliminates transcription errors in financial documents. Creates a complete digital audit trail for every transaction from lease review through execution and archiving. Enables portfolio-wide visibility into lease obligations and upcoming milestones.

patternFlow.dataFlow: Lease PDFs + checklist data → Bronze (raw) → Silver (extracted) → Gold (structured)

patternFlow.dataFlow: Lease PDFs → Azure AI Document Intelligence → extracted lease fields

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint automates real estate transaction processing end-to-end. Lease agreements (PDFs) and checklist data are ingested into the Bronze layer. Azure AI Foundry's document intelligence capabilities extract key lease terms — dates, amounts, parties, clauses — into structured data in the Silver layer. Data quality validation cross-references extracted data against checklists and business rules, flagging discrepancies for human review. Once validated, Power Automate orchestrates the downstream workflow: populating required Excel forms (rent rolls, lease abstracts, approval matrices), routing for digital signatures, sending notifications at each approval stage, and archiving executed documents. Every transaction step is logged for audit compliance. DirectLake dashboards give real estate teams portfolio-wide visibility into lease obligations, upcoming expirations, and financial exposure.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Document extraction accuracy thresholds with human review for low-confidence extractions, Digital signature authority matrix aligned with corporate approval policies, Archiving retention policies per jurisdiction and lease type, Financial data validation against accounting system reconciliation, Access controls restricting lease financial details to authorized personnel, Audit log retention matching legal hold and regulatory requirements
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 10-14 weeks including document intelligence model training, form template development, and signature workflow configuration

useCaseBlueprints.name: Automated Schedule of Learning Sessions
useCaseBlueprints.description: Automates the end-to-end scheduling of learning sessions including instructor-led classes, virtual training, and workshops. Manages room bookings, Teams meeting link generation, facilitator notifications, participant enrollment, and calendar updates through Outlook integration. Provides a unified dashboard of all scheduled sessions with automated reminders and post-session feedback collection.
useCaseBlueprints.businessValue: Eliminates the manual coordination overhead of scheduling learning sessions — room conflicts, missing Teams links, forgotten facilitator notifications. Ensures 100% of sessions have proper resources (rooms, links, materials) booked before participants are notified. Reduces L&D administrative burden by 50-70%, letting the team focus on content quality rather than logistics.

patternFlow.role: Structures learning catalog, session, and enrollment data into managed layers
patternFlow.dataFlow: LMS/course catalog/enrollment data → Bronze → Silver → Gold learning tables

patternFlow.role: Validates session details: facilitator availability, room capacity, time conflicts
patternFlow.dataFlow: Proposed sessions → conflict checks → validation results → cleared/flagged sessions

patternFlow.role: Orchestrates the full scheduling workflow: room booking, Teams link, calendar invites, reminders
patternFlow.dataFlow: Cleared session → book room (Outlook) → create Teams link → send invites → schedule reminders

patternFlow.role: Monitors for schedule changes and triggers update notifications automatically
patternFlow.dataFlow: Session changes → Reflex detection → update notifications to facilitators and participants

patternFlow.role: Provides a unified dashboard of all scheduled sessions, utilization, and attendance
patternFlow.dataFlow: Gold learning data → DirectLake → learning schedule dashboards

patternFlow.role: Tracks L&D KPIs: session utilization, attendance rates, facilitator hours, room usage
patternFlow.dataFlow: Session metrics → Power BI scorecards → goal tracking → trend analysis

useCaseBlueprints.architectureNarrative: This blueprint automates learning session logistics end-to-end. The learning catalog, facilitator profiles, room inventory, and enrollment data are structured through the medallion architecture into a unified Gold layer. When new sessions are proposed, data quality validation checks for conflicts — facilitator double-bookings, room capacity mismatches, time overlaps with existing sessions. Cleared sessions trigger a Power Automate workflow that books the meeting room through Outlook, generates a Teams meeting link, sends calendar invites to facilitators and enrolled participants, and schedules automated reminders (e.g., 1 week, 1 day, 1 hour before). Data Activator monitors for changes (cancellations, room switches, time adjustments) and automatically notifies all affected parties. DirectLake dashboards provide L&D teams with a real-time view of all scheduled sessions, room utilization, and attendance tracking. Metrics scorecards track key L&D KPIs against organizational learning goals.
useCaseBlueprints.requiredGovernance: Facilitator availability calendar integration and conflict resolution rules, Room booking priority rules when conflicts arise, Minimum enrollment thresholds before session confirmation, Cancellation policy enforcement (minimum notice period), Post-session feedback collection and response rate monitoring, Budget tracking for external facilitator costs and room charges
useCaseBlueprints.estimatedTimeline: 6-10 weeks including Outlook/Teams integration, room inventory setup, and notification workflow configuration
